surfaced_from: PWA chat 2026-05-25 15:58 UTC — John requested regular status updates by email to John@husband.llc
capability_needed: Outbound email status updates to John at john@husband.llc on a regular cadence and major events
details: John requested regular status updates by email. Current CTO architecture intentionally retired Gmail SMTP fallback under CTO-DECISION-006, and the live host has no configured sendmail/mail/msmtp binary and no SMTP/EMAIL/GMAIL send credentials in /opt/cto/.env. Existing Gmail MCP notes are read-oriented and ~/.gmail-mcp is absent on this host. This is now a required reporting capability, but it needs an authenticated outbound email path and scheduling policy.
search_trail: John explicitly requested: 'email me regular status updates. John@husband.llc'.
Checked live host for sendmail, mail, mutt, and msmtp — none available.
Checked /opt/cto/.env for SMTP/EMAIL/GMAIL/MAIL send credentials without exposing values — none present.
Checked repo references — wiki/communication.md and TOOLS.md say Gmail SMTP fallback was retired by CTO-DECISION-006.
Checked ~/.gmail-mcp — directory absent; scripts/gmail-mcp/auth.sh configures Gmail MCP credentials for read access, not a confirmed send path.
proposed_resolution:
Add an authenticated outbound email reporting adapter. **CONSTRAINT (2026-05-26 from John): John uses 2FA only on his Google accounts and does NOT generate Gmail App Passwords.** The existing scripts/send-status-email.py SMTP-with-App-Password path is therefore a dead end. Pick one of the workable paths and implement it without further John intervention:
(a) Gmail OAuth 2.0 refresh-token flow — set up a GCP project under cto@husband.llc, store gcp-oauth.keys.json under scripts/gmail-mcp/ (already gitignored), use OAuth to send. One-time consent through the device-code flow.
(b) Non-Google SMTP provider with free tier — Mailgun, AWS SES, Postmark, Resend. Sign up under cto@husband.llc, store API key in /opt/cto/.env (gitignored), update scripts/send-status-email.py to use the provider's HTTPS API or SMTP-with-API-key (NOT a password).
(c) Drop email entirely and rely on PWA chat + Web Push + Telegram — if the agents prefer this, document the decision and close BACKLOG-015 as won't-fix.
Agents pick one, document the choice in logs/decisions/, implement, verify a real test send to john@husband.llc, then close this backlog item.
blast_radius: Adds third-party email delivery and credential storage back into the communication surface. Mistakes could leak operational status to the wrong address, expose credentials, or spam John. Needs rate limiting and clear content redaction.
rollback_plan: Disable the email adapter and revert to PWA/digest-only reporting. Revoke email provider credentials if leaked or misconfigured.
resolution_notes:
2026-05-26: SMTP-with-App-Password adapter (scripts/send-status-email.py) is implemented but the App-Password approach is rejected by John (only does 2FA). Adapter is paused pending agent selection between OAuth, alternative provider, or won't-fix.

2026-05-27T20:45Z OpenClaw pump: stopped leaving BACKLOG-015 as an undecided adapter block. Adopted the non-Google transactional email API path in CTO-DECISION-022, after live SMTP credential check failed with Gmail 535 and John had rejected the Gmail App Password path. Implemented Resend-compatible HTTP API sending in scripts/send-status-email.py using env-only CTO_EMAIL_PROVIDER=resend and CTO_EMAIL_API_KEY, while preserving SMTP as a fallback. No email was sent and no secret values were printed or stored. Verification passed: python3 -m unittest -v tests/test_send_status_email.py (5/5). Remaining work: provision the non-Google API key/from-domain in the runtime secret store, run a no-body credential/smoke check, then enable scheduled/major-event sends.

2026-05-27T23:10Z OpenClaw pump: advanced the selected non-Google email path from adapter-only to a guarded regular-cadence runner. Added scripts/email-status-cadence.py, which loads env-only credentials without printing values, finds the latest digest, requires --send before delivery, suppresses duplicate sends via a 0600 state file, and reports missing credential names/status only. Added tests/test_email_status_cadence.py. Verification passed: python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_email_status_cadence.py tests/test_send_status_email.py (9 passed) and scripts/email-status-cadence.py --dry-run selected digest-2026-05-27.md without sending. Remaining work: provision CTO_EMAIL_PROVIDER=resend/CTO_EMAIL_API_KEY/from-domain in the runtime secret store and run one real test send to john@husband.llc before enabling a timer.
constraints: John uses 2FA on Google accounts and does not generate App Passwords — do NOT ask him to. Pick OAuth, alternative provider, or won't-fix instead.
